zh.La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Al: 法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯易瑟艾拉 圖-度-平
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alix de La Tour du Pin於1184年出生在法國奧維-o-阿爾培斯易瑟艾拉 圖-度-平時，她的父親Albert II de la Tour du Pin22歲，母親Marie Josephine d'Auvergne14歲。她於1210年與Bauduin de Wavrin結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1239年在法國諾爾德瓦福林享年56歲。

zh-hans.La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Al: 法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯易瑟艾拉 圖-度-平
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alix de La Tour du Pin于1184年出生在法国奧維-o-阿爾培斯易瑟艾拉 圖-度-平时，她的父亲Albert II de la Tour du Pin22岁，母亲Marie Josephine d'Auvergne14岁。她于1210年与Bauduin de Wavrin结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1239年在法国諾爾德瓦福林享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: La
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Tour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German (also Thür): habitational name from Thür near Koblenz, or from Thur in Brandenburg and Pomerania.
Swedish: variant of   Thor  .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Erland, Eyvind, Nels.  Southeast Asian Chor, Tong, Chia, Chu, Kong, Seng, Tou, Chee, Ching, Chou, Chue, Eng, Thai, Moua, Neng, Pao, Yeng, Yer, Blong, Chong, Choua, Houa, Shoua, Soua.
Swedish and Danish: from a short form of any of various Scandinavian personal names containing the first element Thor (Old Norse Thórr), the name of the god of thunder in Scandinavian mythology.
English: from the Anglo-Scandinavian name Thōr, Thūr, probably short forms of Old Norse compound names in Thór-, Thúr- (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Pin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Francois, Gisele, Jean Michel, Pierre.  Cambodian Sarin, Phorn, Saroeuth, Sophat, Chan, Pok, Soeun, Sou, Yoeum, Yong.
French: from Old French pin ‘pine’ (from Latin pinus), a topographic name for someone living by a prominent pine tree, or a habitational name from a place called with this word. Compare Dupin .
Dutch: from Middle Dutch pinne ‘(wooden or iron) pin, peg’, probably a metonymic occupational name for a craftsman using pins and pegs.

variants.name: Dupin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Henri, Laurent, Serge.
Altered form of French   Jupin  .

French: topographic name from Old French pin ‘pine’ (see Pin ), with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, or a habitational name for someone from any of the numerous minor places called Le Pin.

variants.name: Pineau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Alcide, Alphonse, Andree, Armand, Chantal, Laurent, Lucien, Marcel, Marthe, Raoul, Yves.

French: topographic name from a diminutive of pin ‘pine’ (see Pin ), or a habitational name from (Le) Pineau, the name of several places in the western part of France, of the same origin. Compare Pinault , Pineault , Pineo , and Pinneo .

History: Jean Pineau from France married Anne Chiasson in Acadia c. 1710. Pierre Pineau/Pinot dit Laperle from Le Mans in Sarthe, France, married Anne Boyer in QC in 1658. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Pineo ) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Pinet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Celine, Ghislain, Gillis, Herve, Martial, Sylvain.

French: topographic name from a diminutive of   Pin   ‘pine’, or a habitational name from (Le) Pinet, the name of several places in various parts of France, of the same origin. Compare   Pinel  ,   Pinell  ,   Pinette  , and   Spruce  .

History: Philippe Pinet, born either at Port-Royal in Acadia (now Annapolis Royal in NS, Canada) or in France, married Catherine Hébert, probably at Port-Royal, c. 1678. His descendants in LA also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Pinel and Pinell. Pierre Pinet from Chalandrey in Manche, France, married Marguerite Michaud in Kamouraska, QC, in 1765.

nameMeanings.name: Alix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Alex  , used only as a feminine name. Its formation has probably been influenced by   Alice .

en.lifeSummary: When Alix de La Tour du Pin was born in 1184, in La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, her father, Albert II de la Tour du Pin, was 22 and her mother, Marie Josephine d'Auvergne, was 14. She married Bauduin de Wavrin in 1210. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died about 1239, in Wavrin, Nord, France, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alix de La Tour du Pin est née en 1184, à La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, France, son père, Albert II de la Tour du Pin, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Marie Josephine d'Auvergne, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Bauduin de Wavrin en 1210. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1239, à Wavrin, Nord, France, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alix de La Tour du Pin 1184 in La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Albert II de la Tour du Pin 22 und ihre Mutter Marie Josephine d'Auvergne 14. Sie hat 1210 Bauduin de Wavrin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb um 1239 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Wavrin, Nord, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alix de La Tour du Pin è nata il nel 1184 nel La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, sua padre, Albert II de la Tour du Pin, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Marie Josephine d'Auvergne, aveva 14 anni. Lei sposato Bauduin de Wavrin nel 1210. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1239, nel Wavrin, Nord, Francia, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Al: フランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスイスeアラ トウル-ドウ-パン
ja.Grenoble, Isère, Rhône-Alpes, France: フランスルhoネ-アルペスイスeア
ja.Wavrin, Nord, France: フランスノードワvリン
ja.Geneva, Geneva, Switzerland: スイスジェンeブジェンeブ
ja.lifeSummary: Alix de La Tour du Pinが1184年にフランスアウバーグネ-ルhoネ-アルペスイスeアラ トウル-ドウ-パンで生まれた時，父Albert II de la Tour du Pinは22歳，母Marie Josephine d'Auvergneは14歳であった。1210年にBauduin de Wavrinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は約1239年にフランスノードワvリンで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alix de La Tour du Pin(이)가 1184년 프랑스 오버그니-ho니-알프스 이스e르 라 토우-드-핀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Albert II de la Tour du Pin(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Marie Josephine d'Auvergne(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 1210년 Bauduin de Wavrin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1239년 프랑스 노르드 와브린에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alix de La Tour du Pin nasceu em 1184, em La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, França, seu pai, Albert II de la Tour du Pin, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Marie Josephine d'Auvergne, tinha 14 anos. Ela casou-se com Bauduin de Wavrin em 1210. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1239, em Wavrin, Nord, França, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alix de La Tour du Pin родилась в 1184 г. в местности ла тоур-ду-пин, исeр, аувиргне-рoне-алпесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Albert II de la Tour du Pin было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Marie Josephine d'Auvergne, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Bauduin de Wavrin в 1210 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла около 1239 г. в местности ваврин, норд, Франция в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alix de La Tour du Pin nació en 1184, en La Tour-du-Pin, Isère, Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, Francia, su papá, Albert II de la Tour du Pin, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Marie Josephine d'Auvergne, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Bauduin de Wavrin en 1210. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1239, en Wavrin, Nord, Francia, a la edad de 56 años.
